relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul returns to Jerusalem with Barnabas and Titus after many years.
He goes by unveiling and protects the work from running into emptiness.
Titus, a Greek, is not compelled into circumcision.
False siblings try to spy out freedom and turn it into slavery.
Paul refuses even an hour of submission so the truth of the good news remains.
Status-faces add nothing where Divine grace has already called.
The good news has a nations-field and a circumcision-field.
The same Divine works in Peter and Paul for different missions.
Pillars recognize grace and extend fellowship.
Remembering the poor remains central.
Paul confronts Cephas directly when table practice contradicts the good news.
Fear causes Cephas to withdraw from table fellowship with the nations.
The hypocrisy spreads and even Barnabas is carried away.
Not walking straight with the good news requires public correction.
Paul speaks from within Judean identity, not outside it.
Being made just comes through Messiah’s faithfulness and trust, not boundary-works of Law.
Messiah is not servant of misalignment.
Rebuilding what freedom tore down exposes transgression.
Through Law, Paul dies to Law so he may live to the Divine.
Crucified with Messiah, the old self no longer rules; Messiah lives in him.
Grace cannot be set aside; if justice comes through Law, Messiah’s death is emptied.

contemplative_note: Galatians 2 is table-freedom fire. The issue is not contempt for Jewish identity, but refusing to make covenant-boundary markers into conditions for shared life in Messiah.
